highlights.text: We were on board for the entirety of it and its thanks to the adherence to two simple principles: 1) Recognizing the footprint of institutional accumulation (the Failed Breakdown).
Institutions long by waiting for a deep elevator down sell, they wait for price to undercut a low, they then trap shorts, price recovers the low, and we squeeze the other way.
This is the Failed Breakdown, and my edge is simply following them.
Then last week, bulls bought every dip via Failed Breakdowns.
I wrote on Tuesday at 4pm: “The Failed Breakdown of the 11:30AM 6818 low is also actionable.” We got exactly this Wednesday - ES went elevator down from 6865 resistance down to 6817.

In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the monster Failed breakdown we had last Wednesday (that took us up 80 points - this is key to learn from) and I’ll talk the actionable plan for Tuesday with entries.

post_title: SPX Puts In Its First Red Day In A Week. Will It Get Bought? December 9th Plan
